stem: A company has multiple business units each with their own AWS accounts, consolidated under AWS Organizations. Which billing benefit does consolidated billing provide?

options.label: A
options.text: Usage from all accounts is combined to reach volume pricing tiers for services like.

options.label: B
options.text: Each account is billed separately, with no pricing benefits

options.label: C
options.text: All accounts share the same IAM users and policies

options.label: D
options.text: Each account receives the free tier separately, doubling the total free usage

options.label: E
options.text: Consolidated billing eliminates data transfer costs between accounts

explanation: With consolidated billing, AWS aggregates usage from all accounts in the organization. If one account uses 30 TB of S3 and another uses 20 TB, the total 50 TB qualifies for volume pricing tiers — a benefit no single account would reach alone.

stem: A startup wants to estimate the monthly AWS cost for a new architecture before building it. Which tool should they use?

options.label: A
options.text: AWS Trusted Advisor — review cost optimization recommendations

options.label: B
options.text: AWS Cost and Usage Report — export billing data for analysis

options.label: C
options.text: AWS Pricing Calculator — model the architecture and estimate monthly costs

options.label: D
options.text: AWS Cost Explorer — analyze current spending trends

options.label: E
options.text: AWS Budgets — set a spending limit before deployment

explanation: AWS Pricing Calculator (calculator.aws) lets you model an architecture by selecting services, regions, and configurations to produce a detailed monthly cost estimate before any resources are deployed.

stem: A company's monthly AWS bill increased by 40% compared to last month. A finance manager asks which service caused the increase. What is the fastest way to identify the cost driver?

options.label: A
options.text: Check AWS Trusted Advisor cost optimization category

options.label: B
options.text: Download the AWS Cost and Usage Report and process it manually

options.label: C
options.text: Contact AWS Support to investigate the bill increase

options.label: D
options.text: Use AWS Cost Explorer to view costs by service for the current.

options.label: E
options.text: Review CloudTrail logs for all API calls made last month

explanation: AWS Cost Explorer's cost breakdown by service view immediately shows which services drove the increase. You can filter by service, linked account, region, and tag to isolate the root cause in minutes.

stem: A company wants to be notified when their monthly AWS spend reaches $10,000 and again when it reaches $15,000. Which service handles this?

options.label: A
options.text: AWS Trusted Advisor — cost category

options.label: B
options.text: Amazon CloudWatch billing alarm

options.label: C
options.text: AWS Cost and Usage Report with S3 delivery

options.label: D
options.text: AWS Cost Explorer with custom reports

options.label: E
options.text: AWS Budgets with alert thresholds

explanation: AWS Budgets lets you create cost budgets with multiple alert thresholds. You can configure alerts at $10,000 (actual) and $15,000 (actual or forecasted), sending notifications via email or SNS.

stem: A company is comparing EC2 purchasing options. They have a workload that must run for the next 12 months without interruption. Which option provides a discount without any upfront commitment?

options.label: A
options.text: On-Demand Instances charge per second or hour with no upfront commitment, providing maximum flexibility but at a higher per-unit cost than Reserved Instances.

options.label: B
options.text: Spot Instances offer up to 90% discount over On-Demand pricing but can be interrupted with two minutes notice, making them unsuitable for steady-state production workloads.

options.label: C
options.text: 1-year Reserved Instances — No Upfront

options.label: D
options.text: 1-year Reserved Instances — All Upfront provides a larger discount than No Upfront in exchange for paying the full reservation cost at the start of the term.

options.label: E
options.text: Dedicated Instances run on hardware dedicated to a single customer for compliance purposes but are charged at a premium and do not offer reservation discounts.

distractors.A: On-Demand Instances charge per second or hour with no upfront commitment, providing maximum flexibility but at a higher per-unit cost than Reserved Instances.
distractors.B: Spot Instances offer up to 90% discount over On-Demand pricing but can be interrupted with two minutes notice, making them unsuitable for steady-state production workloads.
distractors.D: 1-year Reserved Instances — All Upfront provides a larger discount than No Upfront in exchange for paying the full reservation cost at the start of the term.
distractors.E: Dedicated Instances run on hardware dedicated to a single customer for compliance purposes but are charged at a premium and do not offer reservation discounts.

explanation: 1-year Reserved Instances with No Upfront payment commit you to a 1-year term with a discount (~20-40% vs On-Demand) and no upfront payment — just a lower hourly rate for the commitment period.

stem: A company is evaluating AWS support plans. They need access to a Technical Account Manager (TAM) and proactive guidance on architecture reviews. Which plan is required?

options.label: A
options.text: Developer Support

options.label: B
options.text: Enterprise On-Ramp Support

options.label: C
options.text: Enterprise Support

options.label: D
options.text: Basic Support

options.label: E
options.text: Business Support

explanation: AWS Enterprise Support includes a dedicated Technical Account Manager who provides proactive guidance, coordinates Well-Architected Reviews, and serves as a single point of contact for the customer's AWS relationship.

stem: A company's development team regularly forgets to stop EC2 instances on weekends, resulting in unnecessary charges. Which AWS feature can automatically stop these instances outside of business hours?

options.label: A
options.text: AWS Trusted Advisor — automatically terminates unused instances

options.label: B
options.text: AWS Cost Explorer — export a report and manually stop instances

options.label: C
options.text: AWS Budgets — set a spending limit that stops instances when exceeded

options.label: D
options.text: Amazon CloudWatch — alerts when instances have been running too long

options.label: E
options.text: AWS Instance Scheduler (via AWS Systems Manager or EventBridge Scheduler) — schedule start/stop times

explanation: AWS Instance Scheduler (an AWS Solution using EventBridge Scheduler and SSM) or EventBridge Scheduler with Lambda can automatically stop and start EC2 instances on a schedule, eliminating weekend idle costs.

stem: A company uses Amazon S3 to store backups and pays for both storage and data transfer. Which data transfer scenario incurs no cost?

options.label: A
options.text: Transferring data from S3 to an on-premises server via the internet

options.label: B
options.text: Transferring data from S3 to CloudFront in a different Region

options.label: C
options.text: Transferring data between S3 and EC2 in different Regions

options.label: D
options.text: Transferring data from S3 to the internet (public downloads)

options.label: E
options.text: Transferring data from S3 to EC2 in the same Region

explanation: Data transfer between S3 and EC2 within the same AWS Region is free. This is a key architectural reason to co-locate compute and storage in the same Region to minimize data transfer costs.

stem: A company's finance team wants to allocate AWS costs to individual business units. Each unit uses different applications deployed in the same AWS account. What AWS feature enables this cost allocation?

options.label: A
options.text: AWS Organizations — create a separate account per business unit

options.label: B
options.text: Resource tagging with cost allocation tags — tag resources by business unit

options.label: C
options.text: AWS Cost and Usage Report — download billing data and analyze manually

options.label: D
options.text: VPC cost allocation — separate costs by VPC per business unit

options.label: E
options.text: AWS Budgets — create a budget per business unit

explanation: AWS cost allocation tags allow you to tag resources (EC2 instances, RDS databases, S3 buckets) with key-value pairs like BusinessUnit=Marketing. Once activated in the Billing console, these tags appear as dimensions in Cost Explorer, allowing per-unit cost breakdown.

stem: A company has significant Reserved Instance coverage but their instance types are changing due to a modernization project. They want to maintain savings while having flexibility to change instance families. Which option should they purchase instead?

options.label: A
options.text: Standard Reserved Instances for the new instance type

options.label: B
options.text: Dedicated Hosts for maximum control over instance placement

options.label: C
options.text: Spot Instances for the flexibility to change at any time

options.label: D
options.text: On-Demand Instances until the project is complete

options.label: E
options.text: Convertible Reserved Instances that can be exchanged for a different instance type

explanation: Convertible Reserved Instances allow exchange for other instance families, sizes, OSes, or tenancies during the commitment term. They offer less discount than Standard RIs but provide the flexibility needed during architecture changes.

stem: Which of the following is included in the AWS Free Tier for 12 months for new accounts?

options.label: A
options.text: Unlimited Lambda invocations for the first 12 months

options.label: B
options.text: 750 hours per month of Amazon EC2 t2.micro or t3.micro instance usage

options.label: C
options.text: Unlimited EC2 t2.micro usage for the first 12 months

options.label: D
options.text: Free data transfer out to the internet for the first 12 months

options.label: E
options.text: 1 TB per month of S3 storage for the first 12 months

explanation: The AWS 12-month free tier includes 750 hours/month of EC2 t2.micro or t3.micro usage. Since there are ~720 hours in a month, this allows running one instance continuously for free. Additional instances or larger types are charged at standard rates.
